name: Makiki Valley Loop
tagline: Three trails stitched into one perfect loop through the lush valley behind Honolulu — the local's Manoa alternative.
parking: Free parking at the Hawaii Nature Center on Makiki Heights Drive — lot fills by 9am on weekends
bestTime: Early morning for birdsong and cool temps — the canopy keeps it shady all day though
talkStory: The forest you walk through was planted by the CCC in the 1930s — before that, hillsides were nearly bare from cattle ranching and sandalwood harvesting. Young local men during the Depression planted thousands of Cook pines and tropical hardwoods that now form the lush canopy. The Hawaii Nature Center at the trailhead was originally the CCC's base camp. Way fewer people than Manoa Falls with the same jungle vibe — the 3-mile loop is a local favorite.
